name: rf/rectangular_waveguide
description: Hollow TE10 guide two-port: FDTD beta(omega) from arg(S21)/L vs analytic dispersion, gated on extraction conditioning + passivity.
target: FDTD beta from arg(S21)/L within 1% of analytic TE10, GATED on cond(A) + passivity
tolerance_basis: Two-stage (coax_thru precedent): (1) wave-level precondition -- the B=S*A extraction must be well conditioned (cond(A) <= 10) and the solved S passive (max singular value <= 1.05); (2) if met, beta from arg(S21)/L within the pre-registered 1% tolerance (1%-class, coax bench gates its arg(S21)/L beta at 3%). The Yee numerical-dispersion floor at dx=0.02 is 0.095% (computed independently, not tuned to the measurement) and is comfortably inside the committed 1% gate.
falsification: EXECUTED: the extracted beta scales as 1/L, so an assumed L that is 10% wrong shifts beta by ~10% (>> the 1% gate); reverting the selector to the legacy centered operator collapses the injected TE10 sin-correlation to ~0.55 and reddens the mode-shape gate; detuning the matched load to a PEC short spikes |S11| toward unity (tests/rf/wave_validation/test_matched_s11_wave_level.py, green). The gate is falsifiable, mode-shape-checked, and load-discriminating.

metrics.note: TE10 cutoff; band is 1.2 fc .. 2.2 fc (all propagating)

metrics.quantity: beta from arg(S21)/L (median rel error, interior band)
metrics.note: vs analytic TE10 beta=sqrt(k0^2-(pi/a)^2); cond(A)=1.09, max sv=1.001, Yee floor=0.095%
injected mode shape; >= 0.9 confirms a clean half-wave (the Yee-staggered operator delivers 1.0000), so the two-port S-matrix is physical

supporting.note: supporting only; NOT the wave-level exit gate
|d arg(amp_fwd)|/L_ref of the reference TE10 vs analytic dispersion; median 1.21%, max 2.74% over 11 frequencies (normalization-independent)

conservation.a_passive_ratio_note: diagnostic only (not the validity gate; the clean TE10 sits ~0.09)

notes: PASS on the Yee-staggered transverse operator (E1b). The selector injects a clean full-grid TE10 (sin-correlation 1.0000), the terminated two-port S is well conditioned (cond(A) 1.09) and passive (max singular value 1.001), and beta from arg(S21)/L agrees with the analytic TE10 dispersion to 0.05% (median, interior band) at dx=0.02 -- inside the pre-registered 1% gate and the 0.095% Yee floor. This replaces the withdrawn round-3/round-4 BLOCKED record (checkerboard-aliased mode).

Mid-band (1.7-1.8 fc) conservation on the real S-matrix: max singular value 0.05->1.000, 0.025->1.000, 0.02->1.000; reciprocity 0.05->0.0001, 0.025->0.0000, 0.02->0.0000; a_passive/a_driven (diagnostic) 0.05->0.244, 0.025->0.112, 0.02->0.088. Per-tier complex S(f), port a/b and the a_passive ratio spectrum are stored in the artifact 'raw' block (F7c).

External-reference-solver cross-check (TE10 ModeSource, one cloud run at the smallest honest grid): the reference TE10 phase constant from the forward mode amplitudes agrees with the analytic dispersion to 1.21% (median) over 11 frequencies, an independent solver confirmation of the same beta(omega) the FDTD two-port measures. The analytic TE10 dispersion remains the binding first-line reference.
